1: Look! The city at one time was full of people. But now it is completely alone. She was a famous city among the nations of the world. But now she is alone, like a widow. She ruled other countries like a queen. But now she has become a slave.
She weeps aloud in the night. There are tears on her face. She had many lovers. But now there is no one to comfort her. All her friends have turned away from her. They have become her enemies.

Judah's enemies have taken her people away as prisoners. The people suffer as slaves of cruel enemies. Judah's people live among foreign nations. They have nowhere to rest safely. Their enemies chased them and they caught them. Judah's people could not escape.

The roads to Zion are sad places now. Nobody travels along them to come to the festivals. No people meet at the city gates. The priests are sad. The young women weep. The people of Zion are very upset.

Jerusalem's enemies now rule her people. They live an easy life. The Lord has caused Jerusalem to suffer because of the many sins of her people. Enemies have taken her children away as their prisoners.
Zion is not a beautiful city any more. Her leaders are like deer that can find no food to eat. They are too weak to escape from the people who are chasing them.

Jerusalem's people are suffering and they have no homes to live in. So they remember how great their city was, and the valuable things that they had long ago. When their enemies took the city for themselves, nobody came to help them. Jerusalem's enemies laughed while they destroyed the city.

Jerusalem's sins are very bad. That is why it has become an unclean place. At one time, people gave her honour. But now they do not respect her. They have seen her great shame. Jerusalem's people are upset. They hide their faces because they are ashamed.

Everyone can see that Jerusalem has become an unclean place. The people did not think about their sins. They did not worry about what would happen. But a terrible thing happened when enemies destroyed their city. There was nobody who could comfort them. They called to God for help, ‘ Lord, see our trouble! Our enemies are boasting that they have won against us!’

Enemies have taken all Jerusalem's valuable things for themselves. Her people watched as foreigners went into the holy temple. But the Lord himself had said, ‘Foreign people must not go into that special place.’

All Jerusalem's people cry as they look for a bit of food to eat. They have to sell their valuable things to buy enough food to keep them alive. The people say, ‘ Lord, look at us! Nobody respects us!’
They say to those who go past the city, ‘Will you do nothing to help us? Look at us! See that we are suffering very much. The Lord has caused us to suffer because he is very angry with us.

He has sent down fire from above to hurt us. It has destroyed our strength. He has prepared a trap to catch our feet, so that we have to turn back. He has left us weak and alone. We have pain all day, every day.

Our sins seem to be like a yoke around our necks. The Lord himself has tied that heavy load on us. We were strong, but he has caused us to become weak. He has put us under the power of our enemies. We can do nothing to stop them.

The Lord God has removed all the strong fighters who were ready to fight for us. He brought a big army to fight against us, and to destroy our young men. The Lord God has stamped on us, the people of Judah, like people stamp on grapes to make wine.

We weep because these things have happened to us. Tears pour out of our eyes. There is nobody here who can comfort us. Nobody can make us strong and brave. Our enemies have won against us, so our children have no hope.’

Zion's people call out for help, but there is nobody to comfort them. The Lord has decided to punish Jacob's descendants. The nations around Judah have become her enemies. Jerusalem has become like a heap of dirty rubbish among the other nations.

‘The Lord is right to punish us! We have refused to obey his commands. Listen to us, all you nations. See how much we are suffering. Enemies have taken away our young women and our young men as their prisoners.
We called to our lovers to help us. But they refused to help us. Our priests and our leaders died in the city's streets. They were looking for food to keep themselves alive.

Lord, see how much we are suffering! We are very upset. We have turned against you and now we are sad deep inside ourselves. In the streets, our enemies attack us and they kill us. In our homes, there is also death.

People have heard that we are crying, but nobody comes to comfort us. Our enemies know about our great trouble. They are happy to see that you have punished us. Please now make our enemies suffer as we have suffered. You have promised to do that. Please do it soon!
Remember all the wicked things that they have done. Punish them as you have punished us for all our sins. Now we are very sad and we cry a lot. We have no hope.’
2: The Lord God has become very angry. He has caused a dark cloud to cover Zion's people. Jerusalem was the most beautiful city in Israel. But now, from heaven, God has caused it to fall to the ground. He has become so angry, he has not kept his own home safe.

The Lord God has destroyed all the homes of Jacob's descendants. He would not forgive them. Because of his anger, he has destroyed all Judah's strong towns. He has knocked them all down to become heaps of stones. The kingdom and its rulers are now ashamed.

Because of God's great anger, he has completely destroyed Israel's army. When Israel's enemies attacked them, God refused to help them with his power. He destroyed Jacob's descendants, like a fire that burns everything.

He prepared his bow and his arrows, as if he was Israel's enemy. He has used his power to attack us as an enemy! He has killed our brave men that we were proud of. He has become very angry with Zion's people. His anger is like a hot fire that destroys everything.

The Lord God has become like Israel's enemy. He has destroyed Israel. He has destroyed all her palaces. He has caused her strong cities to become heaps of stones. He has caused all Judah's people to weep. They cannot stop crying.

God has knocked down his temple, as if it was a hut in a garden. He has destroyed his special meeting place. The Lord has caused Zion's people to forget the festival days and the Sabbath days. Because he is very angry, he has turned against our king and our priests.

The Lord God no longer accepts sacrifices on his altar. He has gone away from his temple. He has given Jerusalem's palaces to her enemies. They shout loudly in the Lord 's temple, as if it is a happy festival day.

The Lord decided to destroy the strong walls of Zion. He did it carefully, stone by stone, and he did not stop. The towers and the strong walls around the city now lie on the ground. It is very sad to see this.

The gates of the city have fallen down on the ground. God has destroyed the metal bars that lock the gates. The king and his officers are prisoners in other lands. There is nobody to teach God's Law. The prophets no longer receive any visions from the Lord.

Zion's leaders sit on the ground. They are saying nothing. They have dressed themselves in sackcloth. They have put dirt on their heads. The young women of Jerusalem are also sad. They turn their faces down towards the ground.

I have cried so much that my eyes have become weak. Deep inside me, I am very upset. I feel ill because my people are helpless and they are dying. Children and babies fall in the city's streets because they are so weak.
Hungry children ask their mothers, ‘Where is our food and our drink?’ They soon fall down on the streets of the city, like a soldier with a wound. They die slowly in their mothers' arms.

Dear people of Jerusalem, has anyone suffered as much as you have? What can I say that will help you? I cannot comfort you. Your trouble is very great. It is as deep as the sea. Can anyone make you well again?

Your prophets had useless visions that deceived you. They should have told you that your sins were very bad. Then you would have been safe from your trouble. But they deceived you with their messages, which were useless lies.

Everyone who goes past the city laughs at you. They are happy to see your trouble. They insult beautiful Jerusalem and they say, ‘Is this the place that people called the most beautiful city? Did it really bring joy to the whole earth?’
All your enemies laugh at you. They hate you and they insult you. They say ‘We have destroyed this city! We have waited a long time for this day. Now we have finally seen it happen!’

The Lord has done everything that he decided to do. He has done what he promised to do a long time ago. He has destroyed your city and he has not felt sorry about it. He has given your enemies a reason to be happy. He has made them stronger than you.
City of Zion, cry to the Lord God with all your strength. Let your tears pour from your eyes like a river. All day and all night, do not rest. Do not stop weeping.

Get up in the night and call for God's help. Do this all through every night. Tell the Lord God everything that you think and you feel. Let it pour out like water from a jar. Lift up your hands and pray to him. Ask him to save the lives of your children. They fall down on every street in the city because they have no food to eat.

‘Look at us, Lord! You have caused us to suffer more than anyone else! Women eat their own children now! They are the children that they have taken care of. Enemies have killed your priests and your prophets in your own temple!
Young people and old people lie dead in the streets. The enemy's swords have killed our young women and our young men. You killed our people because you were angry, and you did not feel sorry.

You called our enemies to come for a feast! They attacked us from all around. At that time of your great anger, nobody could escape and save their lives. Our enemies have destroyed our own children, the children that we gave birth to and we took care of.’
3: I am the man who has suffered. God punished me because he was angry.
He took me into very dark places. I had to walk in the dark instead of in the light.
He has continued to attack me many times. All day, every day, he punishes me.
He has caused my body and skin to spoil. He has broken my bones.
He has attacked me from every side, so that I have pain and trouble.
He has made me live in a very dark place, like people who died long ago.
He has put heavy chains on me like a prisoner. I cannot escape from my troubles.
I call out loudly for God to help me, but God does not listen to my prayer.
He has used big rocks to build a wall across every road where I go. I cannot go anywhere.
God waits to attack me, like a bear. He hides like a lion that wants to kill.
He has pulled me away from the road. He has torn me in pieces. He has left me without any help.
He is like a soldier who is ready to shoot his arrows. I am the one that he wants to hit!
He has shot his arrows and he has hit me. It hurts me deep inside.
All my people laugh at me. All the time, they sing songs that insult me.
God has caused me to suffer very much, as if I have drunk a bitter drink.
He has made me eat stones that break my teeth. He has pushed me down into the dirt on the ground.
I have no peace in my mind. I cannot remember the happy days that I had before.
I tell myself, ‘I have no strength to live any more. I no longer have hope that the Lord will help me.’
I think about my troubles, with no home to go to. It is like a bitter poison that I have to drink.
I think about these things all the time, and I feel very upset.
But now I remember what God is like, so I still have hope.
The Lord 's faithful love never comes to an end. He never stops being kind to us.
Every day, we can trust him to be kind. Yes God, you are always faithful!
I say to myself, ‘The Lord God is with me and that is enough. I will trust him to help me.’
The Lord is kind to people who trust in him. He helps those who turn to him.
It is good when people wait quietly for the Lord to rescue them.
It is good if you learn to accept trouble when you are still young.
When the Lord causes us to suffer, we should sit alone quietly.
We should be humble as we suffer. Then hope may come again.
If somebody hits us on one side of our face, we should let them hit the other side too. If people insult us, we should accept it.
The Lord God will not turn against his people for ever.
He may cause us to suffer for a time, but then he will be kind to us, because his faithful love is very great.
It does not please him to make people suffer. He does not like to give them pain.
If anyone is cruel to all the prisoners in the land, that is not good.
If anyone turns against the Most High God and cheats people, that is not good.
If anyone tells lies in court, that is not good. The Lord God is not happy when he sees any of these bad things.
Nobody can command things to happen unless the Lord God says it must happen.
The Most High God says what will happen, both good things and bad things.
When God punishes us for our sins we have no reason to complain.
Instead, we should think carefully about how we live. We should turn back to the Lord again.
We should be honest with God in heaven, lift up our hands and pray to him:
‘We have completely turned against you. You have not forgiven us.
You have chased after us with your great anger. You have not felt sorry to kill us.
You have hidden yourself from us in a cloud. So our prayers cannot reach you.
You have caused us to become like dirty rubbish. So the people of other nations do not respect us.
All our enemies are happy to see that we have trouble.
Terrible troubles have come to destroy us. So we are very afraid.’
Tears pour from my eyes like a river, because enemies have destroyed my people.
My tears will continue to pour out. They will never stop,
until the Lord looks down from heaven. He will see what is happening to us.
I see how much the young women of my city are suffering. It makes me very upset.
My enemies had no reason to hate me, but they caught me like people catch birds.
They threw me into a deep hole in the ground and they threw stones at me to kill me.
The water in the hole rose up my head. Then I thought, ‘I will soon die.’
From the bottom of the deep hole I called your name, Lord.
I said, ‘Please listen to me as I call to you for help.’ You heard my prayer.
You came near to me when I prayed to you. You said to me, ‘Do not be afraid.’
Lord God, you spoke on my behalf. You rescued me from death.
You have seen how my enemies have been cruel to me. Judge fairly and show that I am right!
You have seen that my enemies hate me and they make plans to hurt me.
Lord, you have heard them when they insult me. You know about their plans to hurt me.
My enemies say bad things about me. They are always thinking about ways to attack me.
Watch them as they sing songs to insult me. They sing them all the time.
Lord, punish them as they deserve for all the bad things that they have done.
Take away all their hope. Curse them so that they suffer!
Chase after them in your anger. Cause them to disappear from the whole earth.
4: Look! The city's gold is no longer beautiful. Even pure gold has become useless. The jewels from the temple lie on the ground in all the city's streets.
Zion's young men were more valuable to us than gold. But now they seem to have no value. They are like ordinary pots that a potter has made.
Even jackals feed their babies at their breasts. But Jerusalem's young women have become cruel. They are like ostriches that leave their eggs in the desert.
Babies' mouths are completely dry, because they are so thirsty. Young children ask people to give them food, but nobody has any food to share with them.
People who were rich and who ate the best food now die in the streets with nothing to eat. Rich people who wore expensive clothes now die among the rubbish.
God destroyed the city of Sodom in a moment and nobody was there to help them. Now he has punished my people even more than he punished Sodom's people.
The leaders of Jerusalem were very pure, whiter than snow or milk. They were strong and healthy. Their bodies seemed to shine like valuable jewels.
Now their faces are dirty, blacker than coal. When they go out, people do not recognize them. Their skin hangs on their bones. Their skin is as dry as a stick.
It is better for people who died in a battle than for people who have nothing to eat. The fields provided no crops so people died slowly without food.
Women who had been kind mothers have now cooked their own children. Their children became their food, when enemies were attacking the city.
The Lord showed that he was very angry. He started a fire in Zion. The fire destroyed the whole city, even its foundations.
None of the kings of the earth, or the people of any nation could believe it. They never thought that an enemy could march through Jerusalem's gates.
But it happened because of the sins of Jerusalem's prophets and her priests. The city became an unclean place because its leaders caused good people to die there.
They walked around in the city's streets like people who are blind. There was blood on their clothes and people were afraid to touch them.
People shouted at them, ‘Go away! You are unclean! Go away! Do not touch us!’ So they went away to look for homes in other places. But foreign nations said, ‘They cannot stay here with us!’
The Lord himself has sent them away. He does not take care of them any longer. People do not respect the priests. Nobody is kind to the leaders.
Our eyes became weak as we watched to see if help would come. We continued to hope for help from another nation. But no nation was able to rescue us.
Our enemies watched us wherever we went. We could not walk in our own streets. We knew that our end would soon come. Only a few days remained. The time had finally come!
Our enemies could chase us more quickly than eagles that fly high in the sky. They chased us across the mountains. They hid to attack us in the desert.

The king that the Lord had chosen to rule over us was the one who gave us life. We had thought, ‘He will keep us safe from the armies of other nations.’ But now our enemies have caught him in their traps.
Edom's people who live in the land of Uz, You may be happy now. But the Lord will punish you too. You will become drunk and be ashamed with no clothes to wear.

People of Zion, your punishment for your sin will soon finish. God will not leave you to live in foreign countries any longer. But you, Edom's people, God will punish you for your sin. He will show everyone the wicked things that you have done.
5: Lord, please think about what has happened to us. See how ashamed we have become.
Strangers have taken our land for themselves. Foreign people live in our homes.
We have become children without fathers. Our mothers have become widows.
We must pay money for the water that we drink. We must buy expensive wood for our fires.
Our enemies make us work very hard. We are tired and we have no rest.
We have agreed to serve the Egyptians and the Assyrians, so that we could get food to eat.
Our ancestors did wrong things but they are now dead. We receive the punishment that they deserved.
Slaves now rule over us. There is nobody to rescue us from their power.
Robbers hide in the fields, so our lives are in danger when we look for food.
Our skin feels as hot as an oven. We are so hungry that we have a fever.
Our enemies have taken hold of the women in Zion. They have sex with the young women in Judah's cities.
They have hung our rulers by their hands. They have been cruel to our leaders.
They force the young men to work like slaves. Our boys fall down as they carry heavy loads of wood.
Our leaders can no longer meet at the city gate. The young men no longer make their music.
We are not happy any more. We no longer dance, but we weep.
We have nothing to be proud of any more. Terrible trouble will happen to us, because of our sins.
Because of this, we are very upset. Our eyes are full of tears and we cannot see properly.
Mount Zion has become a heap of stones. Wild animals walk all over it.
But Lord, you rule as king for ever. Your kingdom will continue for all time.
Why do you still forget about us? Why have you not come to help us for so long?
Lord, bring us back. Help us to return to you. Let us live well, as we did before.
We are sure that you have not completely turned away from us. Do not be so angry with us that we have no hope.
